name: Hearts of Romaine with Apple Cider Vinaigrette
recipeIngredient: 1 large romaine lettuce, washed and trimmed
4 ounces Stilton cheese, cut into wedges
2 large Pippin apples, cored and quartered
Extra virgin olive oil to coat lightly
1/4 cup organic apple juice, reduced by half
1/4 cup organic apple cider vinegar, or more to taste
1 teaspoon Coleman's dry mustard, or to taste
1/4 cup fresh mixed herbs (basal, tarragon, dill), chopped
recipeInstructions:
For the Salad:
1. Clean romaine by dunking in ice water.
2. Cut off stem end and use only tender inner leaves.
3. Cut Stilton into 1 ounce wedges or crumble.
4. Toast hazelnuts at 300 degrees F until golden brown.
5. Break up slightly or chop lightly.
6. Cut apple wedges into thick slices and toss with a little olive oil seasoned with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
7. Roast apple slices at 350 degrees F until warm but still firm (about 20-30 minutes).
For the Vinaigrette:
1. Place apple juice, vinegar, dry mustard, chutney, and chopped garlic in a food processor.
2. Process until smooth.
3. Add olive oil very slowly until incorporated.
4. Fold in chopped herbs at the last minute.
5. Season to taste with salt and freshly ground black pepper.
Makes about 1 cup of vinaigrette. This vinaigrette will keep up to two weeks in a sealed container in the refrigerator.
